reviews.author: Qu Wenruo
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that the ioctl's return value is not checked for errors, which could lead to silent failures and incorrect results being returned to userspace.

2026-04-17.patch_summary: This patch adds a new ioctl (BTRFS_IOC_GET_CSUMS) to the Btrfs file system, allowing userspace to query the on-disk checksums for a specific file without requiring root privileges. The ioctl takes a struct btrfs_ioctl_get_csums_args containing the offset and length of interest, as well as a buffer to store the results. The kernel returns a struct btrfs_ioctl_get_csums_entry indicating whether the extent has checksums available, along with the checksum data if applicable. This feature is intended to speed up mkfs.btrfs operations by avoiding redundant checksum calculations, particularly when using the --reflink option.

The ioctl takes a struct btrfs_ioctl_get_csums_args containing the offset and length of interest, as well as a buffer to store the results. The kernel returns a struct btrfs_ioctl_get_csums_entry indicating whether the extent has checksums available, along with the checksum data if applicable. This feature is intended to speed up mkfs.btrfs operations by avoiding redundant checksum calculations, particularly when using the --reflink option.

reviews.author: David Sterba
reviews.summary:
Reviewer David Sterba requested that the patch use the check_add_overflow() helper instead of manually checking for overflow, and suggested sending a follow-up patch to implement this change.

Reviewer David Sterba requested that comments be formatted according to the common kernel style, specifically suggesting that each comment should be on its own line and written as a full sentence.

Reviewer noted that the patch is functionally correct and has been added to for-next after applying some coding style fixes.
reviews.raw_body:
I've kept it as is but this should use the check_add_overflow() helper,
please send a followup.

---

Comments should be on separate line and following the common style of a
full sentence.

---

Functionally it seems OK, so I've added it to for-next, with some coding
style fixups. Thanks.

2026-04-21.patch_summary: This patch adds a new ioctl (BTRFS_IOC_GET_CSUMS) to the Btrfs file system, allowing userspace to query the on-disk checksums for a specific file without requiring root privileges. The ioctl takes a struct btrfs_ioctl_get_csums_args containing the offset and length of interest, as well as a buffer to store the results. The kernel returns a struct btrfs_ioctl_get_csums_entry indicating whether the extent has checksums available, along with the checksum data if applicable. This feature is intended to speed up mkfs.btrfs operations by avoiding redundant checksum calculations, particularly when using the --reflink option.
